TranslationContext: This file contains Unit Names for the Deepwood Guardians Faction.

Translations.Comment: Tiefwald = deepwood, Hüter = guardians. Protectors of the ancient forest. 'Bosque Antiguo' emphasizes the timeless, primordial nature of the faction.
Wache = guard. Basic forest defenders. 'Centinela' conveys a watch-like role befitting basic troops.
Standard fantasy term adapted to Spanish. Tree spirits in elven form. Direct transliteration maintains the classic fantasy terminology.
Tänzer = dancers. Swift and graceful forest warriors. 'Danzarines' conveys both the grace and combat prowess of these agile units.
Forest archers. Masters of woodland archery. Direct translation that emphasizes their forest domain.
Waldläufer = rangers. Elite scouts and hunters of the deep forest. 'Merodeadores' captures their roving, elite scout nature.
Fast cavalry of the forest. Riding forest creatures. Classic term for mounted warriors with fantasy flair.
Nature-powered chariot. Full military designation conveys importance and gravitas befitting a powerful unit.
Majestic flying creature of the forest. 'Monumental' emphasizes the size and grandeur of this aerial unit.
Hirsch = stag. Cavalry riding majestic forest stags. Paired term with other mounted units for consistency.
Baumriese = tree giant. Living tree creature. Alternative: Treant (kept as fantasy term). Adapted to Spanish phonetics while maintaining fantasy nomenclature.
Dragon of the forest. Ancient guardian. Classic, straightforward naming befitting the faction's mightiest unit.
Standard fantasy term for gryphon. Spanish adaptation maintains the classic fantasy creature designation.
Noble leader of the forest folk. Direct translation emphasizing both nobility and forest affinity.
Druide = druid. Nature magic user. Spanish form of the universal fantasy term paired with forest affiliation.
Ancient and powerful tree creature. Wise forest spirit. Builds upon 'Treante' translation, emphasizing age and wisdom fitting an elder unit.
